summary: Manual Attendance Import feature testing completed successfully. All 14 backend tests passed, frontend UI verified - Manual Import tab visible with download/upload functionality working correctly.
passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/hr/attendance/template/download returns XLSX with correct headers

Backend: Template has all 14 correct column headers (Employee ID, Name, Dept, Designation, Basic Salary, Housing/Transport/Food/Phone/Other Allowances, Fixed Incentive, Full Days, Half Days, Approved Leaves)
Backend: Template pre-fills active employees with salary structure data
Backend: Template attendance columns (L,M,N) initialized to 0
Backend: Invalid month format returns 400 error
Backend: Import validates total days don't exceed calendar days
Backend: Import creates correct record types (present for full days, half_day flag for half days, leave status)
Backend: Re-importing replaces only manual_import source records
Backend: Payroll endpoint picks up imported attendance data
Backend: Authentication required for template download (403)
Frontend: Manual Import tab visible and accessible
Frontend: Download Template button with data-testid found
Frontend: Upload button with data-testid found
Frontend: Month selector for both download and upload present
Frontend: UI shows proper step-by-step instructions

verification: Returns XLSX file with correct content-type
Headers in row 5 with all 14 columns
Salary columns pre-filled from hr_employees collection
Attendance columns (L,M,N) initialized to 0 for user input
Accepts multipart/form-data with file and month
Validates total days don't exceed calendar days
Creates daily hr_attendance records with source='manual_import'
Full days create present status records
Half days create present status with half_day=true flag
Approved leaves create leave status records
Re-import deletes old manual_import records before inserting new ones
Applies late deductions for half_day records
Correctly computes net salary based on attendance

components: Manual Import tab in Attendance page

critical_code_review_comments: Template download uses openpyxl correctly to generate XLSX with proper styling
Import endpoint properly validates input and creates daily records
Source field 'manual_import' correctly distinguishes from biometric records
Re-import deletes only manual_import records, preserving biometric data
Payroll integration reads from hr_attendance without source filtering (as expected)
Frontend uses proper data-testid attributes for testing
seed_data_creation: Backend tests created attendance records for March, April, May, June 2026 during import testing. These are tagged with source='manual_import'.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Manual Attendance Import feature fully tested. Template download generates XLSX with all active employees and salary breakdowns. Import creates daily hr_attendance records. Existing test data: attendance records exist for months March-June 2026 from import testing. The payroll engine at POST /api/hr/payroll/run correctly reads from hr_attendance collection.
testing_method: Backend pytest (14 tests) + Playwright browser automation for UI verification
